title: Concept of IoT & Architecture | Fill in the Blanks Quiz
heading: Concept of IoT & Architecture - Fill in the Blanks
description: Test your knowledge of Internet of Things (IoT) concepts, architecture layers, design constraints, and analytics maturity levels.

questions.question: In IoT architecture, __________ serve as intelligent intermediaries that perform protocol translation, local data filtering, and temporary storage, while __________ are the physical components responsible for sensing environmental conditions and executing commands.
questions.hint: First blank: bridges between devices and cloud; Second blank: devices that detect and act.

questions.question: Among IoT design constraints, __________ constraints relate to environmental conditions like temperature and moisture, while __________ constraints relate to cost at large scale.
questions.hint: First blank: weather, humidity, physical conditions; Second blank: affordability for mass deployment.

questions.question: In IoT analytics, the __________ level answers 'What will happen?' while the __________ level answers 'What should we do?'.
questions.hint: First blank: forecasting future events; Second blank: recommending actions.

questions.question: In IoT data visualization, __________ visualizations show trends over time, while __________ visualizations display location-based data.
questions.hint: First blank: time-series charts; Second blank: geographical heatmaps or pinpoints.

questions.question: The layer responsible for sensing data in IoT architecture is the __________ layer.
questions.hint: Also called the device or physical layer.

questions.question: The __________ layer is responsible for transmitting data between devices and cloud.
questions.hint: Handles connectivity using protocols like Wi-Fi, LoRa, or 5G.

questions.question: The __________ layer performs data processing and filtering in IoT systems.
questions.hint: Also known as the Edge or Fog layer.

questions.question: The IoT layer that delivers services to end-users is the __________ layer.
questions.hint: Where dashboards, alerts, and user interfaces reside.

questions.question: In IoT, a __________ acts as a bridge between devices and cloud systems.
questions.hint: Performs protocol translation and local preprocessing.

questions.question: The business model where services are provided on subscription basis is called __________.
questions.hint: Anything as a Service (includes SaaS, PaaS, IaaS).

questions.question: In IoT, CapEx stands for __________ expenditure.
questions.hint: One-time investment in physical assets.

questions.question: In IoT, OpEx stands for __________ expenditure.
questions.hint: Recurring costs like maintenance, cloud fees.

questions.question: The architecture that provides a multi-view framework for industrial IoT is called __________.
questions.hint: Industrial Internet Reference Architecture.

questions.question: The four levels of analytics maturity include descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and __________.
questions.hint: The highest level that recommends actions.

questions.question: The 'battery barrier' refers to limitations in __________ supply in IoT devices.
questions.hint: Energy constraints in remote or battery-operated sensors.

questions.question: The principle of integrating security from the beginning is called __________.
questions.hint: Proactive rather than reactive security approach.

questions.question: The DIKW pyramid consists of Data, Information, Knowledge, and __________.
questions.hint: The highest level representing informed decision-making.

questions.question: The five V's of Big Data include Volume, Velocity, Variety, Veracity, and __________.
questions.hint: The ultimate benefit derived from data.

questions.question: Devices that perform actions based on commands are called __________.
questions.hint: They convert electrical signals into physical actions (e.g., motors, relays).

questions.question: A system that automatically takes decisions without human intervention is called __________ control.
questions.hint: Self-governing systems like self-driving cars or automated factories.
